item.title: An opening at Downing Street
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer resignation.

item.title: The indifferent fall of Keir Starmer
item.description: Economic gloom, defense cuts and scandal took down Britain’s prime minister.

item.title: Bowser’s warning to Janeese Lewis George on D.C. fiscal cliff
item.description: The bills may come due sooner than D.C.’s next mayor anticipates.

item.title: Israel feels exposed. Netanyahu could pay for it.
item.description: An interview with one of Israel’s most prominent journalists, Nadav Eyal.

item.title: Alan Greenspan understood power and was not afraid to wield it
item.description: The former Fed chairman’s style offers a lesson in how Washington functions and how institutions grow.

item.title: The fax machine still works. Gather round, friends, and be amazed.
item.description: The old ways are never truly gone.

item.title: I went from a trailer park to the Senate. Architecture matters.
item.description: Planners have turned away from tradition, but boxy concrete doesn’t represent American ideals.

item.title: AI is sparking a boom in blue-collar jobs. Here’s how to fill them.
item.description: Meeting a massive need for more electricians, welders and plumbers will take real ambition.

item.title: The latest data on baby names reveals a progressive paradox
item.description: The affluent may say they dislike convention. But they name their kids Charles, Eleanor and Henry.

item.title: Socialism is back in vogue. Wait for reality to set in.
item.description: Taxing the rich sounds good, until they move away.

item.title: Trump’s payout fund was dropped. Now it’s time to ask questions.
item.description: Readers on immigration court, funding transparency, Congress, federalism and more.
